title: taraxacum protuberans
intro: Taraxacum protuberans, commonly known as the rough dandelion or rough burdock, is a plant species in the dandelion family that has been used for its medicinal and edible properties. It is known for its bitter and astringent properties, which make it useful in treating digestive issues and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and roots have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are also dried for use in capsules or added to food as a nutritious supplement.
